items.title: Rafizi says Bersama aims to break Malaysia’s race-based politics with multiracial platform
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) sees the Johor state election as an opportunity for voters to move away from divisive race-based politics towards a more united platform for Malaysia’s future, said Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. The Bersama co-leader said he hoped the party and its candidates could win support from Malaysians of all races, reflecting the country’s diversity, in today’s polls. Also present was Bersama co-leader Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and the party’s 15 candidates contesting various seats across Johor.

items.title: Four killed in highway crash involving motorcycle convoy and car
items.summary: KUANTAN: Four people were killed and several others injured when a convoy of 12 motorcycles collided with a car at Km 246.4 of the East Coast Highway 1 (LPT1), near the Jabor toll plaza exit today. Three of the motorcyclists, Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, died at the scene. "At the scene, the motorcycle ridden by Che Mohd Sufian suddenly hit the back of the car.

items.title: Ex-Afghan MP extradited to US to face drug, weapons charges
items.summary: Abdul Zahir Qadeer, who was arrested in Nairobi in April of last year, was allegedly a "large-scale international narcotics and weapons trafficker," the department said in a statement. "While purporting to be a political leader of Afghanistan, Abdul Zahir Qadeer was allegedly leading a criminal enterprise dealing in dangerous and addictive narcotics and heavy weapons," Acting US Attorney General Todd Blanche said. "Unbeknownst to Qadeer, that buyer was working with the DEA," Clayton said.

items.title: Vigneswaran confident MIC can retain Indian support in Johor election
items.summary: KULAI, July 11 — MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran is confident that the Indian community will continue to support the Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in the Johor state election tomorrow, especially in the seats contested by MIC. He said that the confidence was based on the good relationship between the MIC and both the federal and state governments in addressing and resolving various issues involving the Indian community. The 16th Johor state election will see four candidates representing the MIC, namely K. Raven Kumar in the Kemelah state assembly seat; V. Rugendran (Kahang); P. Pannir Selvam (Perling); and R. Kumaran (Bukit Batu).

items.title: Anwar hits back at Sanusi over continued attacks despite Kedah development efforts
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, July 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed disappointment over Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor’s continued political attacks against him despite the Federal Government’s ongoing efforts to drive development in the state. When I went to Kedah, he did not even turn up, yet we brought the Thai Prime Minister there (to Bukit Kayu Hitam), not to Kuala Lumpur but to that remote area,” he said when speaking at the PH Grand Finale programme for the Layang-Layang state constituency here last night. He said bringing Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul to Bukit Kayu Hitam yesterday was aimed at officiating the opening of a new road alignment as part of efforts to boost the border economy, thereby benefiting the people of Perlis and Kedah.

items.title: Zahid assures Johor polls outcome will not affect federal govt stability
items.summary: KULAI, July 11 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has given an assurance that whatever the outcome of the 16th Johor State Election today, it will not affect the stability and relationship among political parties at the federal government level. BN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi stressed that the federal administration has so far been functioning as usual and expressed hope that the momentum of harmony would continue without any disruption. The Deputy Prime Minister said ministers and deputy ministers in the federal government have consistently demonstrated professionalism in carrying out their responsibilities without being influenced by political competition in Johor.

items.title: First-time voters queue before dawn to cast ballots in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Two first-time voters queued from as early as 6am to cast their ballots at the Sekolah Kebangsaan Taman Molek polling centre here today. College student Chen Sheng, 21, said he and his friend, Shan Sheng, 21, left Kuala Lumpur at 2am and drove back to Johor Baru to vote. The pair even had time for breakfast before heading to the polling station.

items.title: Anwar’s last push urges Johor voters to back govt that protects people’s interests
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim tonight spearheaded the final push of Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) campaign for the 16th Johor State Election (PRN) through the ‘Grand Finale Johor Ke Depan: Undi Harapan’ programme in Pasir Gudang, just hours before the campaigning period concludes at 11:59 pm last night. Among those in attendance were Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) President Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, who is also the Minister of Agriculture and Food Security; PKR Vice President and Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari; and DAP Secretary-General and Transport Minister Anthony Loke. Merahkan Johor!

items.title: All Johor polling stations open at 8am as state election gets under way
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — A total of 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams for the Johor state election opened simultaneously at 8 am today, allowing voters across the state to elect a new government for the next term. The election sees 172 candidates vying for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats, with 12 four-cornered contests, 27 three-cornered contests and 14 straight fights. For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor.

items.title: Onn Hafiz casts vote in Simpang Renggam
items.summary: KLUANG: Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi arrived at Sekolah Kebangsaan Simpang Renggam this morning to cast his vote in the Johor election. The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman arrived at the polling station at 8.06am, shortly after voting commenced. Clad in a white baju Melayu, Onn Hafiz was greeted by election officials and supporters before proceeding to cast his ballot.

items.title: Meta discontinues AI image feature days after launch
items.summary: CALIFORNIA: Meta said on Friday it is discontinuing an AI feature that allowed users to generate images using public Instagram accounts, days after rolling out the feature. "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," Meta said in a statement. --REUTERS © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Parasite causing 'explosive diarrhoea' sickening Americans
items.summary: NEW YORK: Americans across dozens of states have fallen victim to a microscopic foodborne parasite that can cause "explosive diarrhoea", US health authorities said. The Midwestern state of Michigan has been at the centre of the cyclosporiasis outbreak, with state authorities reporting 1,562 cases as of Friday. Health officials have not identified the cause of contamination that generally stems from fresh produce.

items.title: Oil settles lower on hopes of Strait of Hormuz reopening
items.summary: Brent futures settled at US$76.01 a barrel, down 29 cents, or 0.38 per cent. US West Texas Intermediate crude finished at US$71,41 a barrel down 67 cents or 0.93 per cent. On Thursday, Iranian armed forces launched attacks on US military infrastructure in Gulf states after US strikes on Iran's southern coastal and eastern provinces.

items.title: All 1,076 polling centres across Johor open at 8am
items.summary: JOHOR BATU: A total of 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams for the Johor election opened simultaneously at 8am today, allowing voters across the state to elect a new government for the next term. However, several polling centres in constituencies with a small number of voters will close earlier in stages, beginning as early as 11am. The election sees 172 candidates vying for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats, with 12 four-cornered contests, 27 three-cornered contests and 14 straight fights.

items.title: Shein finally wins China's approval for Hong Kong IPO
items.summary: HONG KONG: Fast-fashion retailer Shein won approval for its long-awaited Hong Kong IPO on Friday, a notice posted on the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) website showed, clearing the way for a listing after failed attempts in New York and London. Shein filed confidentially for its Hong Kong IPO and had not made the filing documents public as of Friday. Shein could now be targeting a valuation of US$40 billion to US$50 billion in its IPO, the source said.

items.title: Lindung 24 contributions in June may not be refunded
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) is unlikely to refund contributions already paid into Lindung 24 Jam, even as it revealed the scheme has collected almost RM58 million since its implementation. Perkeso said contributions made before employees formally opt out from the scheme remain valid under the Employees' Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4). Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said the scheme had been implemented under Act 4 which took effect on June 1, meaning contributions made before an employee formally opts out remain legally valid.

items.title: US delivers first payments to 'Havana Syndrome' victims
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US government has issued the first ever compensation payments to personnel affected by so-called "Havana Syndrome," the mysterious ailment reported by American diplomats in Cuba's capital. In a statement Friday, the Department of Defence said it is "prioritising the care of affected personnel and has disbursed nearly US$3 million in compensation, representing the first Havana Act payments made under any presidential administration." Havana Syndrome was first publicly reported in 2016 when US diplomats on the communist island reported falling ill and hearing piercing sounds at night, sparking speculation of an attack by a foreign entity using an unspecified sonar weapon.

items.title: Cambodian activists demand justice 10 years after Kem Ley murder
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Around 200 Cambodian activists gathered Friday in Phnom Penh to mark the 10th anniversary of the murder of a popular government critic and condemned what they claim is a continued cover-up. Kem Ley, an analyst who dabbled in grassroots politics, was shot at point-blank range at a gas station cafe in the capital in 2016. Around 200 activists, human rights defenders and other supporters rallied Friday at the gas station cafe where they placed flowers in front of Kem Ley's portraits.

items.title: Thai crew sue shipping firm after deadly Hormuz strike
items.summary: BANGKOK: Three former crew members of a Thai cargo ship struck by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz filed a lawsuit on Friday against the vessel's operator and captain for labour violations. Former crew members Panithi Tumkaew, Noppadon Wongsuvan and Suradech Manpuen were dismissed after the incident and suffer from post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) following the attack, their lawyer Kunpat Singhathong said. They filed a lawsuit at the Central Labour Court in Bangkok against the ship's owner, Precious Shipping, two affiliated companies – Precious Flowers and Great Circle Shipping Agency – and the vessel's captain, Sathaporn Hoksee.

items.title: Iran's top negotiator says war with US won't end in 'surrender'
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Friday said the conflict with the United States would not end with Teheran's surrender, after hostilities between the decades-old foes resumed this week with tit-for-tat attacks. "Ending the war is a priority for the countries of the world, but everyone must know that this confrontation will never end with Iran's surrender," said Ghalibaf, who also serves as Iran's parliament speaker, ISNA news agency reported. Iran then said it attacked US assets in Middle Eastern countries with drones and missiles.

items.title: Official account of US agent's fatal shooting of Mexican disputed
items.summary: Houston, July 10, 2026 (AFP) - Witnesses to the fatal shooting of a Mexican man by a US immigration agent in Texas have disputed the official account of events, saying the man never tried to run over the federal officer, their lawyer said Friday. The US Department of Homeland Security said Tuesday that Lorenzo Salgado, 52, had been the target of an operation by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents in Houston and had "attempted to evade arrest." The agency also claimed that Salgado "rammed an ICE law enforcement vehicle...and weaponized his vehicle in an attempt to run over an ICE law enforcement officer resulting in our officer firing his weapon in self-defense."

items.title: Trump sparks US midterm fears with election body firings
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump stood accused Friday of trying to seize control of November's crucial midterm elections after he fired the last two Democrats on a federal agency that oversees fair polls. Trump's brazen removal of the officials from the bipartisan Election Assistance Commission comes as he pushes for voting restrictions ahead of the midterms, in which Republicans could lose their grip on Congress. The two commissioners nominated by Democrats received an email from the Trump administration dismissing them on Thursday, US media reported.

items.title: Mojtaba Khamenei absence signals shifting role for Iran leader
items.summary: PARIS: The absence of Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei from the funeral ceremonies of Ali Khamenei raised questions about his health and fear of assassination, as well as indicating he may perform a quite different function as number one compared with his all-powerful father. Those present included speaker of parliament and chief negotiator in talks with the United States, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, powerful chief justice Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, and Ali Khamenei's eldest son Mostafa Khamenei. But there was no sign of Mojtaba Khamenei, who was appointed supreme leader shortly after his father's killing and has only communicated via written statements since, with no public appearances.

items.title: Nigerian troops kill over 300 'terrorists' in restive north
items.summary: Gusau, Nigeria, July 10, 2026 (AFP) - Nigerian troops killed over 300 members of kidnapping and cattle rustling gangs in the northwestern state of Zamfara this week, a state official said Friday. Abubakar Muhammad, a Gummi resident, said soldiers and local vigilantes fought around 1,000 bandits who were relocating from their camp with hundreds of stolen herd late on Wednesday through Thursday. "The bandits are still under siege by soldiers and vigilantes," Kudi-Kasa told AFP.

items.title: Decision delayed on murder trial over Charlie Kirk killing
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: A US judge said Friday the preliminary hearing into the killing of right-wing influencer Charlie Kirk will be taken up again in September, delaying a decision on whether there is enough evidence to hold a murder trial. The event was planned by Kirk's right-wing youth organisation, Turning Point, which campaigned for President Donald Trump. During this week's hearings, prosecutors presented Robinson's video gamer chats and text messages that they said prove he killed Kirk because he was "fed up" with the "hate" spread by the influencer.

items.title: MMEA, MCBA step up cooperation [WATCH]
items.summary: PORT KLANG: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency and the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) are moving towards closer operational collaboration to strengthen security at Port Klang following a joint maritime patrol earlier this week. Selangor MMEA director Captain Abdul Muhaimin Muhammad Salleh hosted Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal on a patrol covering North Port, South Port and West Port, all of which are under MCBA's jurisdiction. The patrol covered 12 nautical miles off Port Klang here.

items.title: N. Korea military official dumped from ruling party over graft
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un denounced as a "political crime" the alleged corruption of a senior military official dumped from the ruling party for taking bribes, state media reported early Saturday. Kim made the comments at a joint meeting of the Workers' Party of Korea and the army in Pyongyang on Friday, the state news agency KCNA said. The meeting was informed of corruption by Pak Hui Chol, former vice-director in charge of organisational affairs of the General Political Bureau of the People's Army and his followers, the report said.

items.title: Death toll in Venezuela quakes rises past 4,000
items.summary: CARACAS: The death toll in Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has topped 4,000, the government said Friday. At least 4,118 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back June 24 quakes, which flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, Venezuelan parliament chief Jorge Rodriguez wrote on Telegram. Venezuela's interim president Delcy Rodriguez has called for the release of frozen assets held abroad to be used towards the recovery.

items.title: 2.7mil Johor voters head to the polls today
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor takes centre stage today as more than 2.7 million eligible voters are expected to cast their ballots in the 16th state election to choose their representatives for the State Legislative Assembly. Polling stations will open simultaneously at 8am, with 1,076 polling centres and 4,889 voting streams operating across the state to enable voters to decide the mandate for all 56 state seats. To ensure a smooth voting process, the Election Commission (EC) has deployed 43,036 election workers throughout the state.

items.title: D-Day as Johor goes to the polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: After two weeks of intense campaigning, today is "D-Day" as some 2.7 million eligible voters head to the polls to decide who will take the helm of the Johor government. Voters will decide whether they want continuity in the state's development agenda or prefer a state government aligned with the federal administration. The Election Commission is targeting 70 per cent voter turnout, or about 1.8 million voters, for today.

items.title: All eyes on turnout as Johor heads to polls today
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Johor voters head to the polls today in what analysts say will be a closely watched test not only of Barisan Nasional’s (BN) grip on the state, but also of voter enthusiasm ahead of a series of elections expected over the coming years. While BN is widely expected to retain the state government, analysts say the coalition’s margin of victory, voter turnout and the performances of smaller parties could shape the political narrative long after the ballots are counted. Turnout remains the biggest question Political analysts agree that voter turnout will be the single most important factor to watch as polling unfolds.

items.title: US insists Iran commit to stopping attacks in Hormuz strait, say US officials
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States is demanding that Iran publicly state it will stop attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz and that all lanes in the strait will be open to shipping with no tolls, senior US officials said on Friday. The US officials said conversations between the two countries had been productive in recent days. Iran has told Washington that recent attacks on shipping in the strait were from "an errant part of their system," one senior official said.

items.title: Music industry launches AI-generated content labels
items.summary: NEW YORK: Several major music industry organisations on Friday unveiled a labeling system for content created with generative artificial intelligence (AI) that they would like to see widely adopted. The International Federation of the Phonographic Industry (IFPI) and the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA) announced the voluntary labels alongside six other organisations including the Grammys and SAG-AFTRA. They unveiled two labels.

items.title: Taylor Swift paid US$160,000 for New York wedding permit: mayor
items.summary: NEW YORK: Taylor Swift paid $160,000 for a New York City permit to hold her blockbuster wedding with NFL star Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden this month, Mayor Zohran Mamdani said Friday. Mamdani told reporters that the billionaire pop singer covered the bill for this month's star-studded extravaganza, when asked if Swift would pay back the city for police overtime related to the ceremony. "Taylor Swift has paid already the cost of the permit that was lodged, which was over US$160,000 for that event and for the response to that event," Mamdani said.

items.title: Iran denies requesting US talks after Trump says dialogue to continue
items.summary: CAIRO: Iran denied that it had requested negotiations with the United States after President Donald Trump said on Friday that Washington and Teheran had agreed to continue talks despite a recent escalation of hostilities. Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said Tehran had not sought negotiations with the US, but had accepted a visit by the Qatari mediator to Iran, according to Iranian state TV. President Donald Trump on Friday said the United States had agreed to talks with Iran after Tehran asked to continue negotiations, while stressing that the June ceasefire between the two nations was over.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secrets violations
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Apple on Friday sued OpenAI, accusing the AI company of orchestrating a campaign to steal the iPhone maker's trade secrets as it tries to develop its own consumer hardware device. The lawsuit, filed in a federal court in California, paints a picture of an aggressive effort by OpenAI to poach Apple employees and extract confidential information to build its own device. "At every level, from members of its Technical Staff to its Chief Hardware Officer, and in coordination with business partners, OpenAI has been stealing Apple's trade secrets and confidential information," Apple said in the 41-page complaint.

items.title: Trump agrees to more Iran talks but insists truce is over
items.summary: TEHERAN: US President Donald Trump said Friday he had agreed to further negotiations with Iran, even as he repeated his assertion that the ceasefire between the long-time foes was over. While there have been no direct talks between Iran and the United States since last month, Iranian media reported that a delegation from mediator Qatar was in Teheran after the warring sides exchanged strikes again this week. "The Islamic Republic of Iran has asked us to continue 'talks.'

items.title: Anwar tells Johor election rivals to stop using Najib’s imprisonment as campaign issue
items.summary: KULAI, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded parties contesting in the Johor state election to stop using the issue of Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s release as political campaign fodder. “Datuk (Seri) Najib is now in prison, so leave him alone. Merahkan Johor!

items.title: Immigration Dept: 81 officers disciplined over abuse of power, corruption since January
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — A total of 81 Immigration Department officers and rank-and-file personnel faced disciplinary action for abuse of power and corruption between Jan 1 and June 30 this year. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said out of that number, 14 individuals were dismissed from service. “This shows that we are indeed serious about addressing integrity issues among immigration officers.

items.title: Ringgit ends week stronger as safe-haven demand for US dollar fades
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The ringgit ended the week on a stronger note against the US dollar today, as safe-haven demand for the greenback faded. At 6pm, the local currency climbed to 4.0695/0745 against the US dollar from 4.0750/0795 at yesterday’s close. AmBank Group FX strategist Yim Soon Kah told Bernama that the weaker US dollar was driven by reports that US and Iranian mediators would continue peace negotiations despite the recent military escalation, reducing demand for the greenback as a safe-haven currency and for higher-yielding US Treasuries.

items.title: Two men with criminal records detained over Petaling Jaya hotel murder probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Police have arrested two local men to assist in the investigation of the murder of a man who was found dead at a hotel in Sunway Mentari, Petaling Jaya, last Wednesday. Petaling Jaya District Police Chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said that both suspects, in their 20s and 30s, were detained following initial information received by the police at 11:23 pm regarding an unconscious local man at the hotel. “Upon arrival at the scene, police found the victim, in his 30s, lifeless.

items.title: Fahmi heads Penang Governor’s honours list, with Ancom Nylex CEO among recipients
items.summary: Fahmi will be conferred the Darjah Panglima Pangkuan Negeri, which carries the title “Dato’ Seri”, the Penang state government said in a statement today. Another 19 recipients will receive the Darjah Gemilang Pangkuan Negeri, which also carries the “Dato’ Seri” title. A further 72 recipients will receive the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri, which also carries the “Dato’” title.

items.title: Blogger fined RM11,000 over offensive TikTok post targeting Ismail Sabri, Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Sessions Court here fined blogger Jufazli Shi Ahmad RM11,000 today after finding him guilty of transmitting offensive communications against former Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob and UMNO four years ago. “After evaluating the entire defence and the witnesses’ testimonies, the court finds Jufazli guilty and convicts him as charged. Jufazli paid the fine today.

items.title: Fire department prepares 667 assets as Super El Nino raises fire risk from January
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 10 — The Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department has deployed 667 assets nationwide in preparation for the potential impact of the Super El Nino phenomenon, which is expected to affect Malaysia from early next year. Its director-general, Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said all state directors have been instructed to strategically position assets in high-risk areas, particularly in the northern states of Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah’s east coast. “The department has made the necessary preparations, and I have personally met with zone chiefs across the country to review their early preparations before we begin facing the impact of Super El Niño from January to April next year.

items.title: Couple linked to ‘Ah Long’ activities charged over petrol bomb attacks in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH, July 10 — A married couple pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to three charges of mischief, including throwing a petrol bomb (molotov cocktail) causing property damage early last month. M. Dhinakaran and B. Rahtheneswary, both 34 years old, made the plea after all charges were read out in Tamil before Judge Ainul Shahrin Mohamad. Both charges were brought under Section 436 of the Penal Code read together with Section 34 of the same code, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years and a fine, if convicted.

items.title: Kelantan police boost security after graffiti threats, suspicious bag found at shooting range
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 10 — Police have tightened security measures at all police facilities in Kelantan following a series of threats at the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) Kelantan Contingent Shooting Range in Bachok, including deploying security personnel and requesting the installation of closed-circuit television (CCTV) systems. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the measures were taken after acts of vandalism involving graffiti depicting threats were found on the fence of the PDRM Kelantan Contingent Shooting Range, along with the discovery of a black bag in the area believed to be linked to an attempt to intimidate the police. He said that following the incidents, he instructed the Bachok district police chief to station personnel at the Bachok Shooting Range, while also requesting CCTVs to be installed at all police premises to strengthen security and assist investigations.

items.title: Investigation into alleged petroleum cargo document discrepancies at Tanjung Langsat Port still ongoing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The investigation into alleged discrepancies involving petroleum cargo documents at Johor’s Tanjung Langsat Port is still ongoing, according to Maritime Network Sdn Bhd chief executive Datuk Seri R. Jeyenderan. Jeyenderan said he was satisfied with the response received from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) via email in mid-June, which informed him that the case had been received and reviewed by the Information Assessment Committee (JMM), and that investigations were still underway. “We respect the MACC and the investigation process.

items.title: Tomorrow decides Johor’s future, Anwar calls: Return home and vote for fairness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on Johoreans, including those living outside the state, to return and cast their ballots in tomorrow’s state election, describing it as a pivotal moment for Johor’s future. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the election would determine the next chapter in Johor’s story and urged voters to choose leaders who are honest, capable and committed to serving the people. “With humility and respect, I ask you to place your trust in Pakatan Harapan to lead Johor with integrity, sound governance and fairness, ensuring equal opportunities for all,” Anwar wrote.

items.title: Fire Dept: Ammonia leak at Ipoh ice factory caused by unsecured cylinder; Hazmat team contains spill
items.summary: IPOH, July 10 — A 400-litre ammonia gas cylinder, which was believed to have not been properly closed, caused a gas leak and chemical spill at an ice manufacturing factory in the Menglembu Industrial Area here today. He said the leak was caused by the gas cylinder that was not properly closed, resulting in uncontrolled gas pressure release and a spillage of about seven litres of ammonia. Sabarodzi said two Hazmat members, fully equipped with personal protective equipment (PPE), then entered the risk area to close the valve on the gas cylinder, thereby stopping the gas release.

items.title: RTM implements strict SOP to filter LGBT content across TV, radio and digital platforms, says Communications Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Department of Broadcasting Malaysia (RTM) has implemented a strict standard operating procedure (SOP) to ensure all content produced, acquired, and broadcast on its television, radio, and digital platforms complies with established broadcasting guidelines. The Ministry of Communications said the SOP requires filtering lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) content in all local and foreign programmes to prevent broadcasting materials that may negatively affect society, especially children. The ministry added that all local and international content, including children’s animated products, on its television channels must undergo quality control (QC) by the Creative Content Unit (UKK).

items.title: Home Ministry outlines projects to improve security, efficiency and services in Johor, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — The Home Ministry (KDN) will continue to focus on implementing various development projects in Johor to strengthen security infrastructure, enhance operational efficiency and improve public service delivery in the state. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said Johor is an important state in the southern region of the country given its role as one of the nation’s key hubs for security, public services and international entry points. He said the development projects involve several agencies under the ministry, including the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Immigration Department, the National Registration Department (JPN), the Prisons Department, the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) and the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA).

items.title: Firefighters battle Kuching ice cream factory blaze spreading to neighbouring premises, all workers safe
items.summary: KUCHING, July 10 — A fire broke out at an ice cream factory and spread to a neighbouring factory at Demak Laut Industrial Park here on Friday afternoon. Firefighters from the Tabuan Jaya and Petra Jaya fire stations are at the scene battling the blaze after receiving a distress call at 12.23pm. “Firefighters are at the scene to extinguish the fire,” the Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) said.

items.title: Johor polls: Give Pakatan a chance, Hannah Yeoh urges Pekan Nanas voters
items.summary: PONTIAN, July 10 — DAP deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh has called on voters in the Pekan Nanas state constituency to give Pakatan Harapan (PH) the opportunity to serve the area once again. “(PH candidate for the Pekan Nanas state seat) Yeo Tung Siong is an experienced candidate who understands the appropriate channels and agencies to engage in resolving the people’s issues. N55: Pekan Nanas — 37,556 voters 1.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand in technical talks to address sea bass and prawn trade concerns, says Fisheries D-G
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — Malaysia and Thailand are in talks to address concerns surrounding the trade of sea bass and prawns, with both countries working towards an amicable resolution. Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said the discussions focused on enhancing technical requirements to facilitate trade without compromising biosecurity, food safety and aquatic animal health. Adnan said Malaysia had submitted 35 technical questions concerning the prawn trade to seek further clarification following an initial assessment of the information provided by Thailand, before finalising any risk assessment.

items.title: Johor DAP deputy chairman Sheikh Umar apologises for riding without helmet, says he will pay the fine
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, July 10 — Johor DAP deputy chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali has apologised after being spotted riding a motorcycle without a helmet during nomination day in Paloh. I apologise for riding a motorcycle without a helmet on nomination morning in Paloh. Loke also shared a photograph of Barisan Nasional campaigners, including a former minister, similarly riding without helmets.

items.title: MetMalaysia forecasts morning rain, thunderstorms in several Johor districts on polling day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — Several areas in Johor are expected to experience rain and thunderstorms tomorrow, polling day for the 16th Johor state election, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). Based on the weather forecast published on the department’s official website today, rain is expected in the morning in Johor Bahru, Batu Pahat, Kulai, Muar, Pontian and Tangkak. However, no rain is forecast in the six districts in the afternoon and at night.

items.title: Johor polls: Anthony Loke hopes voter turnout will exceed 70pc, cites strong demand for buses and trains
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 10 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke hopes voter turnout in the Johor state election tomorrow will exceed 70 per cent to ensure that the government elected truly reflects the voice of the majority of the people. In the last state election, he said, voter turnout was around 54 per cent due to border closures that prevented Malaysians working in Singapore from returning home to vote. At the event, Loke also announced Negeri Sembilan state executive councillor J. Arul Kumar’s research officer, S. Mugunthan, 40, as the party’s new candidate for the Seremban Jaya state seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election.

items.title: Johor polls enter final campaign stretch as 2.7 million voters prepare to elect 56 assemblymen tomorrow
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 10 — After two weeks of intense campaigning, the curtain will fall on the 16th Johor state election campaign at 11.59 pm tonight, bringing to an end all canvassing activities, both on the ground and online. Overall voter turnout in the 2022 Johor state election stood at 54.92 per cent. Party flags are seen in Johor Bahru ahead of the 16th Johor state election on July 11.

items.title: EAIC report: Citizenship approvals for seven naturalised players processed too quickly, not orderly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — The Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) has found irregularities in the citizenship approval process for seven naturalised Malaysian footballers and has urged the Home Ministry and related agencies to tighten procedures and introduce clear guidelines. The commission said it received complaints over the approvals and subsequently set up a special task force under Section 17 of the Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission Act 2009 to investigate. The probe involved the National Registration Department (NRD), Immigration Department, Royal Malaysia Police and the Football Association of Malaysia, with statements taken from 15 officers and five civilian witnesses.

items.title: Johor polls: EC says online campaign‑material complaints should go directly to MCMC
items.summary: PONTIAN, July 10 — The Election Commission (EC) has called on the public to report online posters or campaign materials unrelated to candidates in the Johor state election directly to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). EC Chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the commission’s enforcement team had removed physical campaign materials at certain locations following complaints. “However, campaign materials distributed via online platforms fall under MCMC’s jurisdiction.
